events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: roofing work, nail placement high/exposed. This is a classic defect in asphalt shingle roofing where nails are placed too high on the shingle (above the seal line or exposed to weather) rather than in the proper nail line. This is non-compliant with IRC R905 and manufacturer specs. High/exposed nails can lead to wind uplift, leaks, and shingle blow-off.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (roofing failure, water intrusion, wind damage)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 85-90% since roughed in but defective
Confidence: high, 0.9
